user.description:
Set up your Eaton UPS with Network-M2 or Network-M3 card. Certificates will be generated automatically.

After setup, check **Settings → System → Repairs** for instructions to upload the client certificate to your UPS.

reauth_confirm.description: Re-authenticate your Eaton UPS connection. Leave certificate fields empty to auto-generate new certificates.

reconfigure.description: Reconfigure your Eaton UPS connection. Leave certificate fields empty to auto-generate new certificates.

error.connection: Unable to connect to the server.
error.cert_fetch_failed: Failed to fetch or generate certificates. Check that the host is reachable.
error.host_unreachable: No route to host. Check that the IP address is correct and the device is on the network.
error.connection_refused: Connection refused. Check that the port is correct and MQTT is enabled on the UPS.
error.connection_timeout: Connection timed out. The host may be unreachable or a firewall is blocking the port.
error.server_cert_mismatch: Server certificate does not match. Try clearing the certificate fields to fetch a new one.
error.tls_handshake_failed: TLS handshake failed. The UPS may not trust the client certificate — check that it has been uploaded.
error.mqtt_connect_refused: MQTT broker refused the connection. Check that MQTT is enabled on the UPS.
error.no_data_received: Connected to the UPS but received no identification data within the timeout.
error.invalid_response: Connected to the UPS but received an invalid response.

cert_upload_required.title: Upload client certificate to Eaton UPS
